ucs.title: [LLM] CopyKittens Matryoshka loader: rundll32 + hidden-window PowerShell DNS C2 chain
ucs.description: Hunts the signature CopyKittens (Iranian, G0052) execution chain seen in Operation Wilted Tulip where rundll32.exe sideloads the Matryoshka v1/v2 DLL backdoor and then spawns powershell.exe with -WindowStyle Hidden / -EncodedCommand to stage Cobalt Strike or Empire over DNS. Combines T1218.011, T1059.001 and T1564.003 in a single parent-child sequence rarely seen in benign software.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as image values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name=rundll32.exe Processes.process_name IN (powershell.exe,pwsh.exe) (Processes.process="*-w*hidden*" OR Processes.process="*WindowStyle*Hidden*" OR Processes.process="*-enc*" OR Processes.process="*EncodedCommand*" OR Processes.process="*FromBase64String*" OR Processes.process="*-nop*" OR Processes.process="*-noni*") by host Processes.parent_process Processes.process_name Processes.process_id Processes.parent_process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename host as src | join type=left src process_id [| tstats summariesonly=t values(All_Traffic.dest) as c2_dest values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as c2_port from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where (All_Traffic.dest_port=53 OR All_Traffic.dest_port=443) by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.process_id | rename All_Traffic.src as src All_Traffic.process_id as process_id] | where isnotnull(c2_dest) | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let suspiciousPS = dynamic(["-w hidden","-windowstyle hidden","-enc ","-encodedcommand","frombase64string","-nop ","-noni","downloadstring","iex(","invoke-expression"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "rundll32.exe"
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (suspiciousPS)
| extend rundll_cmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine
// Matryoshka style: rundll32 executing a non-System DLL or a quoted DllMain export
| where rundll_cmd !contains "\\system32\\" or rundll_cmd matches regex @"(?i)rundll32(\.exe)?\s+[\"']?[A-Z]:\\Users\\"
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where RemotePort in (53,443) and ActionType == "ConnectionSuccess"
    | project DeviceId, NetTime=Timestamp, ConnectingPID=InitiatingProcessId, RemoteIP, RemoteUrl, RemotePort
) on DeviceId
| where ProcessId == ConnectingPID and NetTime between (Timestamp .. Timestamp + 5m)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, rundll_cmd, ProcessCommandLine, RemoteIP, RemoteUrl, RemotePort
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Operation Wilted Tulip (ClearSky/Trend Micro 2017) documented Matryoshka and TDTESS being loaded specifically via rundll32 calling an exported function in a user-writable directory, which then spawned hidden PowerShell to retrieve Cobalt Strike Beacon over DNS/HTTPS. The triple combination of rundll32 → hidden PowerShell → outbound 53/443 in <5 min is highly atypical of legitimate Windows behavior

ucs.title: [LLM] CopyKittens code-signing abuse: execution of binaries signed by AI Squared / known stolen certs + WinRAR staging
ucs.description: Detects the CopyKittens habit (G0052) of signing Matryoshka and TDTESS payloads with stolen or fraudulently obtained certificates (e.g. AI Squared Inc.) and then using WinRAR/7-Zip with a password to stage data for exfil — chaining T1553.002 with T1560.001. This sequence was a hallmark of Operation Wilted Tulip targeting Israeli and Saudi diplomatic entities.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.process_path) as path from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN (rar.exe,winrar.exe,7z.exe,7za.exe) (Processes.process="*a *" OR Processes.process="* a -*") (Processes.process="*-hp*" OR Processes.process="*-p*" OR Processes.process="*-m5*" OR Processes.process="*-v*") by host Processes.user Processes.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename host as dest | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=t values(Processes.process_name) as signed_proc values(Processes.process_path) as signed_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_signature IN ("AI Squared Inc*","Egis Technology*","Pacnet Internet*") OR Processes.process_signature_verified=false) Processes.process_path IN ("*\\Users\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\AppData\\*") by host Processes.process_name | rename host as dest] | where isnotnull(signed_proc) | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
ucs.kql:
let copykittens_certs = dynamic(["AI Squared Inc","AI Squared, Inc.","Egis Technology","Pacnet Internet"]);
let suspiciousSign =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where (InitiatingProcessSignerType in ("Unsigned","Unknown") and FolderPath has_any (@"\Users\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\AppData\"))
        or InitiatingProcessSignatureStatus in ("Invalid","Expired","Revoked")
        or InitiatingProcessPublisher has_any (copykittens_certs)
    | project SignTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, SignedProc=FileName, SignedPath=FolderPath, Publisher=InitiatingProcessPublisher;
let archiveStage =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where FileName in~ ("rar.exe","winrar.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe")
    | where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s+a\s+" 
        and (ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-hp"," -p") or ProcessCommandLine has "-m5" or ProcessCommandLine has "-v")
    | project ArchTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, ProcessCommandLine, ArchPath=FolderPath;
suspiciousSign
| join kind=inner archiveStage on DeviceId
| where ArchTime between (SignTime .. SignTime + 24h)
| project ArchTime, DeviceName, AccountName, Publisher, SignedProc, SignedPath, ProcessCommandLine
| order by ArchTime desc
ucs.rationale: ClearSky's Wilted Tulip report explicitly named the AI Squared Inc. certificate that CopyKittens abused to sign Matryoshka droppers, and follow-on collection used WinRAR with -hp password protection before exfil over their HTTP/DNS proxies (T1090). Pivoting from a stolen-cert binary executing in a user-writable path to a password-protected archive command on the same host within 24 hours is a beha
